summary: Wishlist Collections — Bulk Move feature tested end-to-end. Backend 12/13 pass (1 minor edge case: ?collection_id= empty string is treated literally instead of None). Frontend mobile flow PASSES every step in the request: chips row renders with counts, '+ New list' creates DiwaliUI, Select mode + 'Move to list (N)' button works, modal lists All-saved + every collection, tapping a target triggers the correct toast ('Moved 2 items to DiwaliUI'), selection mode exits, chip count updates to 2, filter shows only those 2 cards, and round-tripping back to 'All saved (no list)' also works with toast. No red-screen errors after expo restart (pre-restart stale bundle showed 'toast is not defined' — confirmed already fixed in source).

minor.issue: Empty-string collection_id is matched literally against documents (returns 0 results) instead of being treated as None per spec. In routers/wishlist.py line 49, the check is `if collection_id is not None:` — for FastAPI Query() this fails because empty string is a non-None str. Easy fix: `if collection_id:`. Frontend currently avoids passing empty string so users are unaffected.

issues: When wishlist has >=2 in-stock items, the orange 'Move all (N)' pill (testID wishlist-move-all-btn) can overlap the small trash 'Clear all' button on narrow viewports — purely cosmetic, not blocking.

action_items: (Optional, minor) routers/wishlist.py:49 — change `if collection_id is not None:` to `if collection_id:` so empty-string query param is treated as None per spec.
(Cosmetic) Consider hiding 'Move all (N)' button when toolbar is cramped, or moving 'Clear all' to a kebab/menu to avoid overlap.
critical_code_review_comments: wishlist.tsx (1137 lines) is approaching the 700-line guideline; consider extracting modals (sort sheet, new-list, move-to-list) and chip row into sub-components for maintainability.

moveSelectedToCollection issues N PATCH requests in parallel (Promise.all over selected.size). Works fine for small selections but a bulk endpoint POST /api/wishlist/items/move-bulk { product_ids, collection_id } would be more efficient and atomic — worth considering later.
Backend PATCH /api/wishlist/items/{product_id} correctly validates collection ownership (line 429-434) and returns 404 for missing collection / missing item — verified by tests.
seed_data_creation: Test creates `Diwali TEST` collection (auto-cleaned in fixture teardown) and uses 3 seeded products from /api/products. UI test creates a `DiwaliUI` collection — leftover collections are wiped before each run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: test_wishlist_collections_move.py covers the collection move feature comprehensively. Backend BASE_URL resolved from EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL. The single failing assertion (empty-string collection_id) is a one-line backend fix if main agent wants to honour the spec literally; frontend doesn't trigger it.
rca_of_issue: Stale Metro bundle initially served the pre-fix wishlist.tsx that referenced `toast.show(...)` causing 'toast is not defined' ReferenceError and a red-screen on /wishlist. `sudo supervisorctl restart expo` rebuilt the bundle and the error disappeared — the fix already in source (uses destructured `show` from useToast) is correct. Verified with grep: no `toast` token remains in /app/frontend/app/wishlist.tsx.
